content:
Beginner Guide: Basic Git and GitHub Commands # Git is a distributed version control system used to track changes in code, while GitHub is a platform that allows developers to host and collaborate on Git repositories.
This guide introduces the most important Git and GitHub commands that every beginner should know.
1. Configure Git # Before using Git, you should configure your username and email.
git config --global user.name "OneTime24" git config --global user.email "onetimehexa@gmail.com"
title: Beginner Guide: Basic Git and GitHub Commands

content:
Basic Git and GitHub Commands # Here is a list of essential commands for working with Git and GitHub:
1. Git Configuration # git config --global user.name "Your Name" git config --global user.email "you@example.com"
title: Basic Git and GitHub Commands
